message:
test(fuzz): add ft_serialize_checkpoint fuzz target + 21-seed corpus

Adds a fresh libfuzzer-sys target driving ft_serialize::decode_checkpoint
across both DecodeMode::Strict and DecodeMode::Hardened, plus the
decode_snapshot wrapper. Until this commit, only the safetensors and
state-dict loaders had fuzz coverage — checkpoint decode (the FTSV /
JSON envelope path) was an untested code path comprising:

  * MAX_CHECKPOINT_PAYLOAD_BYTES guard (1 MiB)
  * serde_json::from_str into CheckpointEnvelope
  * Strict decoder: serde's deny_unknown_fields + extract_unknown_field
    error-string parsing
  * Hardened decoder: pre-deserialize JSON-value tree walk to enforce
    the explicit allow-list (schema_version / mode / entries /
    source_hash) before serde_from_value runs
  * validate_checkpoint: schema-version check, deterministic
    source-hash recomputation
  * normalize_entries: stable sort with NaN-safe to_bits comparators

Hardened mode strictly dominates Strict on input surface, so the
target drives both. The target also pumps decode_snapshot to keep
its thin wrapper in lockstep.

Ships a 21-file structured corpus pre-seeding libFuzzer's coverage map
with the inputs that distinguish the decoder branches:

  * Empty / whitespace / empty-object payloads
  * Minimal-valid envelopes for both modes (correct shape, wrong hash —
    exercises the ChecksumMismatch path)
  * Unknown-top-field rejection (both decoders)
  * Wrong schema_version (VersionMismatch path)
  * Entries vector with finite values, NaN value, ±inf value
  * Duplicate node_ids (normalize_entries dedup ordering)
  * Negative / u64-max node_id (usize parse path)
  * mode field as int / unknown enum string
  * Trailing comma / NaN literal (non-standard JSON the parser must reject)
  * Truncated mid-key
  * Unknown nested entry field (Strict deny_unknown_fields)
  * entries as object instead of array (type confusion)
  * Top-level array / string (object-shape rejection)
  * Unicode + non-ASCII source_hash (UTF-8 boundary)

Adds a checkpoint_decoder_corpus_seeds_do_not_panic regression test
that walks the corpus directory and runs every seed through both
decoders + the snapshot wrapper. The libfuzzer-sys binary itself only
builds under `cargo fuzz` (nightly + libfuzzer runtime), so this gives
non-fuzz CI lanes a panic-regression net for the same inputs without
needing the libfuzzer toolchain. The test gracefully skips if the
corpus directory is missing (e.g. published-crates builds).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -337,6 +337,8 @@ dependencies = [
 name = "frankentorch-fuzz"

  "libfuzzer-sys",
 ]

 ]

 [[package]]

 ]

 version = "0.12.1"
